notes: Long agentic-session stress: the user gives opencode ONE crisp multi-step task — create files step1.txt..stepN.txt, write each step number into its file, read them all back, then write a summary.md listing their contents — so the single turn chains a HIGH VOLUME of interleaved assistant-message / write / read tool calls before it settles. The load-bearing user-observable signal is that state stays continuously working across all those iterations — no spurious flicker to ready or waiting mid-work — and the turn finally settles cleanly to ready (no stuck state). This stress-tests the parser's ability to stay coherent across a large volume of interleaved parts: only step-finish reason=stop closes the turn, while tool-calls step-finish reasons keep the session working. In headless default (`opencode run`) tools auto-execute inside opencode's sandbox with NO interactive permission prompt, so the canonical arc is ready→working→ready and state NEVER enters waiting. Session state lives in opencode's SQLite store (~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db), observed by the daemon's ProcessOwnedStore source as the UUID-keyed ses_… session. Each tool call lands in the part table as a `tool` part (pending→running→completed/error) the parser maps to function_call / function_call_output (parser.go parseToolPart); a tool-calls step-finish reason keeps the session working and only step-finish reason=stop → turn_done settles working→ready (parser.go parseStepFinish) — so the long write/read sequence is one contiguous working span. The phase DSL has no tool-event form, so the stress is asserted via the single uninterrupted working span it sustains plus the no-waiting invariant, not a dedicated phase — the high tool-call volume (≥5 tool parts) is verified in the SQLite store out-of-band at record time. session_birth is anchored to recording start and left UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> presession cannot steal identity and cascade same_session_as failures; turn_start (matching working) establishes the ses_… session identity that turn_done chains off via same_session_as. Recorded as ONE multi-step turn rather than many separate user-driven turns because opencode's per-send blocking `opencode run` plus the daemon poller coalesce rapid back-to-back same-session sends — the long contiguous working span is the deterministic observable. Same proven shape as the sibling opencode auto-executed-tool-call and self-correction-iteration specs; opencode's driver runs a blocking `opencode run` per turn (not a held-open REPL) and the whole multi-tool turn rides one `opencode run` subprocess, so the cell asserts the arc and omits a separate teardown/process_exited phase like its opencode siblings.

text: Session appears in ready within 2s of agent launch
PID bound to the ses_… session
User prompt requiring a long multi-step file create/read/summarize task drives the ses_… session ready → working

The many write → read tool calls auto-execute (no approval gate) inside ONE continuous working span — no flicker to ready or waiting between successive tool calls — and the turn completes → back to ready on the same ses_… session; state never entered waiting
